proposition: The Ohio Supreme Court in Kelley articulated the physical-control test before the legislature codified it: a person is in physical control when they are in the driver's seat, behind the steering wheel, in possession of the ignition key, and physically capable of starting the engine.
quoted_text: ["in the driver's seat of a vehicle, behind the steering wheel, in possession of the ignition key, and in such condition that he is physically capable of starting the engine and causing the vehicle to move"]

proposition: A separate 'operation' theory under ORC 4511.19 could still apply if the State proved the person drove to a spot before climbing over to the passenger seat. Mackie discusses the contours of 'operation.'
cited_case: State v. Mackie, 128 Ohio App. 3d 167 (1st Dist. 1998)
